summary: Dashboard workspace composition with persistent destination navigation, section navigation, optional tools, and one main content surface.
useWhen: The product has multiple persistent destinations and route-level sections.
The workspace needs coordinated panel, bar, and tool chrome around changing page content.
avoidWhen: The experience is a focused single-page flow without persistent application navigation.
A marketing or editorial layout needs independent page composition.

components.role: Coordinates workspace layout and shared chrome.
Provides route-section tabs or the current section heading.
Provides optional workspace tools and their drawer content.

stateOwnership: The host router owns the active destination and section.
The host owns tool drawer open state and active tool content.
AppShell coordinates layout metrics, chrome style, gradient alignment, and shell shortcuts.
accessibility: Render one main landmark in the default content slot.
Use navigation components for destinations and sections rather than repurposing command buttons.
Provide accessible labels for icon-only tool actions.
responsiveBehavior: Allow PanelNav to collapse while preserving the content region.
Keep BarNav section access available through its overflow behavior.
Treat PanelTools as optional chrome and avoid shrinking main content below its usable minimum.
